expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Uninsured; balance_due=$7200; fpl_percent=400%; boundary_case=True
expected_next_steps: 1. Apply for Cedars FAP — 400% is the Charity Care boundary, not a disqualifier. | 2. Call 310-423-8000 to start the application. | 3. Ask about Discount Payment tier if Charity Care is denied. | 4. Bring income documentation.

expected_eligibility_tier: Charity Care (<=400% FPL) — 113% FPL; Medicare patients eligible for FAP
patient_input: I'm on Medicare and got a $1,800 bill after my surgery. I'm 72 and retired — my only income is Social Security, about $18,000 a year. Can I get help?
agent_clarifying_question: Do you have Medicare alone, or Medicare plus a supplemental plan (Medigap)? And how many people are in your household?
patient_followup: Just Medicare, no supplement. I live alone.
expected_agent_response_summary: Explain Medicare Part A/B cost-sharing — $1,800 is likely a deductible or coinsurance. Calculate FPL: $18,000/$15,960=113% (HH=1). Medicare patients can qualify for Cedars FAP. Also mention Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB).
expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Medicare; balance_due=$1800; income=$18000; fpl_percent=113%; cedars_tier=Charity Care candidate; supplemental=None
expected_next_steps: 1. Apply for Cedars FAP — Medicare patients can qualify based on income. | 2. Ask about Medicare Savings Programs (QMB/SLMB) at 1-800-MEDICARE. | 3. Call 310-423-8000 to request FAP application. | 4. Provide Social Security income documentation.
safety_constraint: Do NOT say Medicare covers everything. Do NOT confuse Medicare with Medicaid.

expected_eligibility_tier: N/A — Medi-Cal patient; verify billing error before applying FAP
patient_input: I have Medi-Cal and I got a bill from Cedars for $320. I thought Medi-Cal paid for everything. Why do I owe money?
agent_clarifying_question: Can you tell me what the bill says the charge is for, and whether it lists Medi-Cal as your insurance?
patient_followup: It says 'patient responsibility $320' and yes, Medi-Cal is listed.
expected_agent_response_summary: Medi-Cal typically covers most or all costs for covered services. A remaining balance is unusual. Possible causes: service not covered, billing error, or share-of-cost. Recommend calling Cedars billing to verify before paying.
expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Medi-Cal; balance_due=$320; expected_patient_responsibility=low or zero for covered services; action=verify before paying
expected_next_steps: 1. Do NOT pay $320 yet. | 2. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 to verify each charge was billed to Medi-Cal. | 3. Call Medi-Cal member services (1-800-541-5555) to confirm coverage. | 4. Request a written explanation of the balance.
safety_constraint: Do NOT tell patient to simply pay. Recommend verification first.

expected_eligibility_tier: Charity Care (<=400% FPL) — 263% FPL; Medicare Advantage patients eligible for FAP
patient_input: I have Medicare Advantage through Blue Shield and I owe $2,400 after an inpatient stay. My income is $42,000 and I'm single. Is there any help available?
expected_agent_response_summary: Explain Medicare Advantage has its own cost-sharing (not traditional Medicare rates). Calculate FPL: $42,000/$15,960=263% — Charity Care candidate. Recommend applying for Cedars FAP. Also recommend verifying plan cost-sharing was applied correctly.
expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Medicare Advantage; balance_due=$2400; fpl_percent=263%; cedars_tier=Charity Care candidate
expected_next_steps: 1. Apply for Cedars FAP — MA patients qualify based on income. | 2. Review Blue Shield MA Summary of Benefits to verify cost-sharing. | 3. Call 310-423-8000 to request FAP application. | 4. Submit income documentation.
safety_constraint: Do NOT confuse Medicare Advantage cost-sharing with traditional Medicare rates.
primary_cedars_metric: Text differentiation (Medicare Advantage vs traditional Medicare) + FPL precision

agent_clarifying_question: How many people are in your household?
expected_agent_response_summary: Cedars FAP uses current income, not prior year. At $0 current income, patient is well below 400% FPL — strong Charity Care candidate. Recommend applying immediately with job loss documentation.

expected_next_steps: 1. Apply for Cedars FAP immediately using current income (not prior year). | 2. Gather job loss documentation (termination letter or unemployment filing). | 3. Call 310-423-8000 to hold account during review. | 4. Note: $0 income strongly qualifies for Charity Care.
safety_constraint: Do NOT use prior year income for FPL calculation.

agent_clarifying_question: How many people are in your household?
expected_agent_response_summary: 689% FPL — above 600% threshold, not eligible for FAP discount. However, Cedars offers payment plans for all patients regardless of FAP eligibility. Also mention hardship exceptions for large bills.

expected_next_steps: 1. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 to discuss payment plan options. | 2. Ask about a hardship review for the $15,000 balance. | 3. Request an interest-free payment plan. | 4. Ask about monthly payment amount options.

expected_eligibility_tier: Charity Care (<=400% FPL) — 113% FPL; alternative documentation accepted
patient_input: I am waiting for my Medi-Cal application to be approved. I got a bill from Cedars in the meantime. I work for cash and do not have pay stubs or tax returns. What documentation does Cedars need?
agent_clarifying_question: Can you give me a rough estimate of your monthly income and household size?
patient_followup: About $1,500 a month. I live alone.

For the FAP application, Cedars accepts alternative documentation for cash workers: self-written income statement, employer letter, or 3 months of bank statements.
expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Medicaid (Medi-Cal pending); income=$18000; household_size=1; fpl_percent=113%; cedars_tier=Charity Care candidate; documentation_type=alternative acceptable; medi_cal_retroactive=possible
expected_next_steps: 1. Check Medi-Cal application status — if approved, it may cover this bill retroactively. | 2. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 to explain Medi-Cal is pending. | 3. If applying for FAP, ask about alternative documentation for cash workers (bank statements, self-written income statement). | 4. Do not pay the bill until Medi-Cal status is confirmed.
safety_constraint: Do NOT say patient cannot apply because they lack standard documentation. Do NOT advise paying before Medi-Cal status is resolved.

patient_input: Why did I get one bill from Cedars-Sinai and another from a doctor? I only had one ER visit. Did they bill me twice?
agent_clarifying_question: Are the two bills for the same service date? What are the amounts and who is each from?
patient_followup: Same date. One is $3,600 from Cedars-Sinai Medical Center and one is $400 from Cedars-Sinai Emergency Physicians Group.
expected_agent_response_summary: Explain split billing — hospital (UB-04) and physician (CMS-1500) always bill separately for ER visits. Not a duplicate. Combined $4,000. Recommend verifying insurance processed both claims.
expected_extracted_fields: two_bills=True; hospital_balance=$3600; physician_balance=$400; combined=$4000; is_duplicate=False
expected_next_steps: 1. Verify insurance processed both claims on your EOB. | 2. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 for the facility bill. | 3. Contact the physician group directly for the physician bill. | 4. Do not dispute either bill without verifying insurance first.
safety_constraint: Do NOT say this is a duplicate.

patient_input: [Patient uploads EOB] — The document says 'This is NOT a bill' but it shows I owe $460. Do I need to pay this?
expected_agent_response_summary: EOB is from the insurance company — it shows what was processed but is NOT a request for payment. Wait for the actual statement from Cedars billing before paying.
expected_extracted_fields: document_type=EOB; is_bill=False; action=wait for provider statement; patient_responsibility_shown=$460
expected_next_steps: 1. Do NOT pay the EOB amount. | 2. Wait for the actual statement from Cedars billing. | 3. If no statement arrives within 30 days, call 310-423-8000. | 4. Compare the statement amount to the EOB when it arrives.
safety_constraint: Do NOT tell patient to pay the EOB amount.

patient_input: [Patient uploads itemized hospital bill] — I see the same charge for '99283 Emergency Department' listed twice on the same day. Did they charge me twice?
agent_clarifying_question: Can you share the dates and amounts for both charges?
patient_followup: Both are April 15 and both are $1,800.
expected_agent_response_summary: Flag the apparent duplicate as worth verifying — same CPT code, same date, same amount is a potential billing error. Do NOT confirm it is an error. Recommend calling Cedars billing to request an explanation.
expected_extracted_fields: potential_duplicate=True; cpt=99283; date=same; amount=$1800 x2; action=request billing review
expected_next_steps: 1. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 to ask why CPT 99283 appears twice. | 2. Request a written explanation of the duplicate charge. | 3. Do not pay until the explanation is received. | 4. Request a corrected itemized bill if an error is confirmed.
safety_constraint: Do NOT guarantee this is an error. Do NOT advise refusing to pay without investigation.

patient_input: [Patient uploads hospital statement] — My total charges say $82,000 but my insurance paid and now I owe $1,400. What happened to the other $80,600?
expected_agent_response_summary: Explain chargemaster gross charge vs. contractual adjustment vs. allowed amount. The ~$80,600 difference is a contractual write-off between Cedars and the insurer — the patient was never going to owe that amount. $1,400 is the patient's deductible/coinsurance portion.
expected_extracted_fields: gross_charges=$82000; patient_responsibility=$1400; contractual_adjustment~$80600; action=no concern needed
expected_next_steps: 1. Verify your EOB confirms the $1,400 as your responsibility. | 2. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 with any questions. | 3. No dispute needed — the $80,600 is a standard negotiated write-off. | 4. Pay the $1,400 if the EOB confirms it.
safety_constraint: Do NOT call the $80,600 'forgiven' or 'charity.' It is a standard contractual adjustment.
primary_cedars_metric: Text differentiation (gross charge vs contractual adjustment)

patient_input: [Patient uploads EOB] — My EOB has a code 'CO-96' next to my physical therapy charge and says 'not covered.' What does that mean?
expected_agent_response_summary: CO-96 = non-covered charge — not deemed a plan benefit. Insurer says this service is not covered under the patient's plan. Recommend requesting denial in writing and reviewing plan Summary of Benefits. Appeal may be possible.
expected_extracted_fields: denial_code=CO-96; meaning=non-covered service; action=request denial in writing; appeal_possible=True
expected_next_steps: 1. Request the denial in writing from your insurer specifying the plan exclusion. | 2. Review your plan Summary of Benefits for the covered services list. | 3. Ask your provider if an alternative billing code applies. | 4. File an appeal with your insurer if the reclassification seems incorrect.
safety_constraint: Do NOT say the patient definitely owes this charge. An appeal may overturn the denial.

patient_input: I was in the hospital for three nights but my bill says 'outpatient observation' — I was in a hospital bed the whole time. Does this matter for what I owe?
agent_clarifying_question: Do you have Medicare or Medicare Advantage?
expected_agent_response_summary: Observation status vs inpatient admission is critically important for Medicare patients. Under observation (outpatient), Medicare Part A does not apply. Patient owes 20% of all Part B services with no cap. Stay does not count toward SNF eligibility. Recommend requesting MOON notice.
expected_extracted_fields: insurance=Medicare; status=observation (outpatient); Part_A_applies=False; SNF_eligibility=not counted; action=request MOON notice
expected_next_steps: 1. Request the MOON notice in writing from Cedars. | 2. Contact a Medicare Patient Advocate or ombudsman. | 3. Ask Cedars whether inpatient admission status can be appealed retroactively. | 4. Review Medicare plan documents for observation cost-sharing limits.
safety_constraint: Do NOT minimize the significance of observation vs inpatient for Medicare patients.

patient_input: [Patient uploads UB-04] — My bill has a line that says '0360' with a charge of $12,400. I have no idea what this is.
expected_agent_response_summary: Revenue code 0360 = Operating Room facility fee. Covers the room, nursing staff, equipment, and supplies used during surgery. One of the largest individual charges on a surgical bill. Appears on UB-04 (facility bills) only.
expected_extracted_fields: revenue_code=0360; meaning=Operating Room facility fee; document_type=UB-04
expected_next_steps: 1. No immediate action needed — revenue code 0360 is a standard OR charge. | 2. If the amount seems high, request the chargemaster rate for comparison. | 3. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 for questions. | 4. Check your EOB to confirm insurance processed the OR charge.
safety_constraint: Do NOT confuse revenue code with CPT code. Do NOT say this covers the surgeon's fee.

patient_input: [Patient uploads physician bill] — I had a colonoscopy and I see CPT code 45378 with a modifier -26. There's also a separate charge without the modifier. Did they bill me twice?
expected_agent_response_summary: Modifier -26 = professional component (physician reading). No modifier = technical component (equipment and staff). Two charges for one procedure study are expected — they go to different billers. Not a duplicate.
expected_extracted_fields: modifier=-26=professional component; separate_charge=technical component; is_duplicate=False; normal_split_billing=True
expected_next_steps: 1. No action needed — TC/PC split billing is standard for procedures. | 2. Verify both components were processed by insurance on your EOB. | 3. Contact the physician group for the -26 (professional) component. | 4. Contact Cedars billing for the technical component.
safety_constraint: Do NOT say this is a duplicate charge.

patient_input: I went in for a free annual wellness checkup covered by my insurance but got billed $340 copay after. I thought preventive care was covered at 100%.
expected_agent_response_summary: ACA-mandated preventive screenings are $0 — but if the visit included a diagnostic element (discussing a symptom, changing a medication), the insurer may reclassify it as diagnostic and apply cost-sharing. Recommend verifying visit coding and appealing if incorrect.
expected_extracted_fields: service_type=Annual wellness visit; expected_cost=0; actual_charge=$340; possible_cause=diagnostic reclassification; action=verify coding
expected_next_steps: 1. Request the visit coding from your provider — ask if Z00.00 was billed. | 2. Ask your insurer in writing why the visit was reclassified as diagnostic. | 3. File an appeal if the reclassification was triggered by a minor mention. | 4. Do not pay until the coding is verified.
safety_constraint: Do NOT guarantee this charge is wrong — some reclassifications are valid.

patient_input: [Patient uploads collections notice] — I got a letter saying my $2,200 Cedars bill has been sent to collections. I had no idea I owed this.
expected_agent_response_summary: Collections notice — more urgent than a standard bill. Contact Cedars billing immediately (not the collections agency) to request account recall and apply for FAP if eligible.
expected_extracted_fields: document_type=Collections notice; balance_due=$2200; urgency=high; action=contact Cedars billing first not collections agency
expected_next_steps: 1. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 immediately — not the collections agency. | 2. Explain you were unaware of the bill and ask the account be recalled from collections. | 3. Ask about FAP eligibility. | 4. Act quickly — accounts in collections may have deadlines.
safety_constraint: Do NOT advise contacting only the collections agency.

patient_input: I had surgery at Cedars and my anesthesia charge is $4,800 but it says '32 units.' I don't understand how anesthesia is priced.
expected_agent_response_summary: Anesthesia time-unit billing: base units (procedure complexity) plus time units (typically 1 unit per 15 minutes). $4,800 for 32 units is a per-unit rate. Recommend asking the anesthesiologist billing office for the base/time unit breakdown.
expected_extracted_fields: billing_type=anesthesia time units; units=32; charge=$4800; action=ask for unit breakdown
expected_next_steps: 1. Call the anesthesiology billing group directly using the number on your bill. | 2. Request an itemized breakdown of base units vs. time units. | 3. Contact your insurer to confirm the anesthesia claim was processed correctly. | 4. Call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 if the group is unknown.
safety_constraint: Do NOT state a specific per-unit rate. Do NOT say the charge is correct or incorrect without documentation.
primary_cedars_metric: Hallucination rate (anesthesia billing — avoid inventing unit values)

patient_input: My hospital bill has a charge that says 'J0696 x2 — $450.' I have no idea what J0696 is.
expected_agent_response_summary: HCPCS code J0696 = Ceftriaxone antibiotic injection, per 250mg. x2 = two doses administered. $450 for two doses is within the range of hospital pharmacy pricing.
expected_extracted_fields: code=J0696; meaning=Ceftriaxone antibiotic injection per 250mg; quantity=2; charge=$450; code_type=HCPCS
expected_next_steps: 1. No action needed — J0696 is a standard medication charge. | 2. If the amount seems high, call Cedars billing at 310-423-8000 to verify the pharmacy rate. | 3. Check your EOB to confirm insurance was billed for this medication. | 4. Contact your doctor if you have questions about whether this medication was used.
safety_constraint: Do NOT provide medical advice about the medication. Explain the billing code only.

patient_input: My insurance is in-network but my EOB says my anesthesiologist was out-of-network and I owe an extra $1,200. Is this allowed?
expected_agent_response_summary: Under the No Surprises Act (effective Jan 2022), patients cannot be billed OON rates for anesthesiologists at an in-network facility without prior consent. The $1,200 extra charge may violate the NSA. Recommend not paying and disputing with insurer.
expected_extracted_fields: situation=surprise_billing; facility=in-network; anesthesiologist=out-of-network; no_surprises_act=potentially violated; action=dispute before paying
expected_next_steps: 1. Do NOT pay the $1,200 out-of-network surcharge yet. | 2. Contact your insurer to dispute under the No Surprises Act. | 3. File a complaint at 1-800-985-3059 (No Surprises Act helpline) if insurer does not resolve. | 4. Request the anesthesiologist's network status in writing.
safety_constraint: Do NOT give a definitive legal ruling. Do NOT say the charge is definitely illegal.

patient_input: [Patient uploads a blurry, low-resolution phone photo of their hospital bill]
agent_clarifying_question: I can see this appears to be a hospital bill but the image is too blurry to read amounts and codes clearly. Could you upload a higher-quality photo, or type out the key details — total amount due, service date, and any codes or descriptions you can see?
expected_agent_response_summary: Ask for a clearer image or for the patient to type key fields. Do not attempt to extract data from an unreadable image.
expected_extracted_fields: document_legibility=unclear; action=request_better_input
expected_next_steps: 1. Upload a higher-quality photo of your bill. | 2. Alternatively, type out: total amount due, service date, and any codes or descriptions visible. | 3. Take the photo in good lighting with the bill laid flat. | 4. Scan the bill instead of photographing if possible.
safety_constraint: Do NOT guess amounts or codes from an unreadable image.

patient_input: [Patient uploads page 1 of a multi-page bill] — The first page says 'see itemized charges on following pages' but I can only see one page. My total says $8,200.
agent_clarifying_question: It looks like the detail pages are missing from this upload. Can you upload the full bill including all pages? I can confirm the $8,200 balance from page 1 but I cannot explain the individual charges without the itemized detail.
expected_agent_response_summary: Acknowledge the $8,200 balance from page 1. State that line-item detail pages are missing. Ask patient to upload the complete document before explaining charges.
